projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: Stable presence of 12-14 devices with zero camera activity on a Monday mid-morning confirms the household is home together today, likely due to a day off or remote schedule, so standard weekday absence automations should be suppressed.

The combination of consistently high device presence (17-20) and zero camera activity on a Monday morning strongly suggests the house is empty, with family devices still lingering on the Wi-Fi network. This is worth noting to confirm everyone is safely out or to check if any devices were left behind.

The sustained 16–21 device count combined with zero camera motion at nearly 2 AM on a Monday strongly indicates an unreported sleepover or large group of guests, as the device count far exceeds the four-person household baseline.

The consistent 16–19 device count paired with zero camera motion across the entire 22:00–00:45 window confirms the household is fully settled indoors with no late-night activity or departures, validating a stable overnight routine.

The sustained presence of 16–19 mobile devices combined with zero camera motion at 10:47 PM strongly indicates a large, settled indoor gathering (likely guests or a family event) rather than just the core household, confirming the home is fully occupied and calm.

The sustained mobile device count of 18–22 over the past four hours confirms the earlier surge was a large group of guests who are still present for a Sunday evening gathering, rather than a temporary spike or network anomaly.

The sustained mobile device count of 16–20 over the past four hours confirms the earlier surge was a prolonged guest gathering rather than a transient spike, indicating approximately 10–14 extra visitors are still present in the home.

Mobile device count surged from ~13 to ~21 between 17:01 and 17:41, strongly indicating a group of guests has arrived for a Sunday evening gathering. The absence of corresponding camera activity suggests entry cameras may not be triggering or are offline, so a quick verification of front-door security is recommended.

The persistent presence of 13-18 mobile devices across the entire morning, continuing the already flagged early spike, strongly suggests unaccounted guests or visitors are still present. This warrants awareness for security and household management.

Network presence consistently shows 17-19 connected mobile devices from 05:40 to 07:19, which is notably higher than expected for your household. Combined with zero camera activity, this may indicate guests or a family gathering, though it could also reflect typical high device usage.
